topic: An employee is asked to train their replacement
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the conference room felt thick, suffocating. Sarah stared at Mark, her jaw tight enough to crack. "So, you're expecting me to just hand over the keys to the kingdom?" Her voice was dangerously low, each word clipped and sharp. She gripped the edge of the mahogany table until her knuckles bleached white. "This isn't a casual handover, Mark. This is handing over years of accumulated knowledge, processes, and institutional memory. Are you even capable of absorbing any of it?" A muscle twitched violently in her neck. She took a slow, deliberate breath, trying to keep the tremor out of her hands. "I'll do it. But don't mistake competence for compliance."

[story 2]

"Train me," the manager had said, his tone maddeningly cheerful. Now, standing in the sterile cubicle, Alex felt a hot flush creeping up his neck. He slammed the binder shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet office. He paced three steps to the window, his shoulders hunched, his breathing shallow and fast. *This is ridiculous. After everything I’ve put in,* he thought, his eyes burning with an intense, frustrated heat. "Look," Alex managed, his voice strained, "I can show you the ropes. But don't expect me to sugarcoat the inefficiencies. I’m not going to pretend this is some pleasant little orientation session. I expect you to pay attention, or you’ll be learning the hard way."

[story 3]

When David was assigned the task, a cold, hard knot formed in his stomach that radiated outward. He watched the new hire, eager and brightly smiling, taking notes with an almost alarming enthusiasm. David felt a sudden, intense need to shatter something—anything. He leaned against the filing cabinet, his arms crossed so tightly his biceps strained against the fabric of his shirt. "Let's be clear," David said, his tone flat, devoid of any warmth. "I'm not here to play babysitter. I'm here to transfer operational control. If you find my methods inconvenient, you'll need to address that directly. Because I won't be bending over backward to make things easy for you."
